title: Promotion with Check
setup: A white pawn stands one square from glory on **a7**, with the black king lined up nearby.
hint: Promote with check whenever possible — it gives Black no time to react.
explanation:
## The Idea

A classic **promotion** tactic. The pawn on a7 reaches the eighth rank and transforms into a queen, simultaneously giving check on the 8th rank.

White plays {{a8=Q+}}.

## Black's Forced Reply

The new queen on a8 attacks the king on e8 along the **eighth rank**. Black must move the king (no piece to block, queen far away from kingside defenders):

- {{Kd7}}, {{Ke7}}, {{Kf7}}, {{Kf8}}, {{Kd8}} — any flight square works since the queen is alone.

## Why Promotion with Check Matters

> Promoting with check **denies Black a tempo**. A non-checking promotion can be answered by counter-threats; a checking promotion forces an immediate king move.

In endgames, a single tempo is often the difference between winning and drawing. Always look for checks when promoting — they are golden.
